title: ageratina paupercula
intro: Ageratina paupercula, also known as skunkbush or skunkbrush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive, unpleasant odor when bruised. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ageratina paupercula include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Ageratina paupercula include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
